rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Pleuroapical scarring, greater on the right. Centrilobular emphysema in the upper lobes.  
Linear atelectasis in the right middle lobe, lingula, and in lung bases bilaterally.

A few solid pulmonary nodules are present in series 5 as follows:

In the right lung:
- In the middle lobe:
  - Subpleural nodule measuring up to 5 mm (image 150).
- In the lower lobe:
  - Nodule measuring up to 4 mm (image 137).
In the left lung:
- In the upper lobe:
  - Nodule measuring up to 4 mm (image 90), calcified.
In the right upper lobe, areas of fibrotic bands at the level of images 36-85 (series 5) with a few calcifications up to 7 mm.  
No pleural effusion.  
The trachea and main bronchi are patent.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No masses or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly.  
No significant pericardial effusion.  
Aortic valve calcification.  
Mild coronary calcification (left anterior descending artery). The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated, with wall calcification.  
The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation.  
Transpedicular fixation at T1-T2. Decreased density of bone tissue in the vertebrae at T11-L1 to 110 HU.

Upper abdomen: Unremarkable.  
Included soft tissues at the level of the examination: In the breasts (partially included in the scan range), multiple partially calcified lesions measuring up to 9 mm. At the T5 level, a subcutaneous lesion in the interscapular region measures up to 9 × 8 mm with density of approximately +19 HU.
rad_impression_en:
1. A few solid pulmonary nodules. Given the presence of risk factors, follow-up CT of the chest may be performed in 12 months per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations. Pulmonary emphysema. Fibrotic bands with a few calcifications in the right upper lobe, possibly post-inflammatory (including post-tuberculous) in etiology; correlate with clinical history.

2. Mild coronary calcification. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

3. Aortic valve calcification.

4. Atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta.

5. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation. Transpedicular fixation at T1-T2. Decreased vertebral body density (CT signs of demineralization) at T11-L1.

6. Multiple calcified lesions in the breasts (partially included in the scan range) (oleogranulomas?); correlate with preferred imaging modalities. Subcutaneous lesion in the interscapular region at T5, possibly an atheroma; correlate with physical examination.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Bilateral pleuroapical scarring. In the apices of both lungs there are several areas of paraseptal emphysema. In the upper lobes and apices, there is moderate centrilobular emphysema. In both lungs there are scattered few cysts with lower lobe predominance. In the upper lobe of the right lung there are several ground glass areas with reticulations, bronchiectasis, strands to the pleura and emphysema at the periphery. The largest ground glass area measures 3.3 × 2.2 cm (image 70, series 5) with a few calcifications up to 7 mm. The ground glass area on image 46 measures 1.7 × 1.3 cm (image 70, series 5). Linear atelectasis in the right middle lobe, lingula, and in lung bases. There are gravitational changes bilaterally. In the right lower lobe there is a paravertebral area of osteophyte-induced fibrosis. A few solid pulmonary nodules are present in series 5 as follows: 
In the right middle lobe:
  - Subpleural solid nodule measures up to 5 mm (image 150).
In the right lower lobe:
  - Solid nodule measures up to 4 mm (image 137).
In the left upper lobe:
  - Calcified nodule measuring up to 4 mm (image 90).
No pleural effusion. The trachea and main bronchi are patent. Mild bronchial wall thickening.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No masses or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No significant pericardial effusion. Mild mitral valve calcification. Coronary artery calcifications are identified in the left main, left anterior descending, and right coronary arteries, qualitatively assessed as moderate.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated, with moderate wall calcification. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation. Transpedicular fixation at T1-T2. Decreased density of bone tissue in the vertebrae at T11-L1 to 110 HU (CT signs of demineralization). Degenerative changes in the left shoulder joint. In the breasts, multiple partially calcified lesions measure up to 9 mm, likely late fat necrosis. Correlate with the dedicated breast imaging.

Upper abdomen: In the lateral crus of the left adrenal gland there is a lesion of 1.1 cm along the short axis with a density -9 HU.
val_impression_en:
1. In the upper lobe of the right lung there are ground glass areas with reticulations and bronchiectasis, with a few calcifications, may correspond to fibrous changes (post-inflammatory). ACR Fleischner Society recommendations (MacMahon, et al. Radiology 2017; 284(1): 228-43) suggest the following: Initial follow-up chest CT at 3-6 months to confirm persistence. If stable, follow-up chest CT every 2 years until 5 years. 
Two solid nodules of the right lung up to 5 mm. ACR Fleischner Society recommendations (MacMahon, et al. Radiology 2017; 284(1): 228-43) suggest the following: For patients with high risk of lung cancer, generally no need for follow-up. An optional follow-up chest CT at 12 months could be performed if there is high index of suspicion. If unchanged, no further follow-up is necessary.

2. Mild paraseptal emphysema, moderate centrilobular emphysema. Pulmonary emphysema on CT is an independent risk factor for lung cancer. Consider enrollment in a low-dose CT (LDCT) lung cancer screening program if the patient meets eligibility criteria; today's CT may serve as a baseline for future screening. Note: Recommendation applies unless the patient has a history of lung cancer, known pulmonary nodules or lung mass, receives routine chest CTs/screening, or is receiving palliative care.

3. Scattered pulmonary cysts may be clinically insignificant or consistent with the signs of lymphangioleiomyomatosis.

4. Coronary artery calcifications are identified in the left main, left anterior descending, and right coronary arteries, qualitatively assessed as moderate. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

5. Mild mitral valve calcification. Atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta.

6. Left adrenal lesion with benign features (adenoma). ACR White Paper recommendations (Mayo-Smith, et al. J Am Coll Radiol 2017;14:1038-1044) suggest the following: Benign adenoma. No need for further imaging follow-up. Consider biochemical evaluation to determine functional status.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.

rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Heterogeneous lung aeration due to alternating areas of mosaic ground-glass opacity and areas of paraseptal and centrilobular emphysema, greater at the apices, and panlobular emphysema along the dorsal surfaces of the bilateral lower lobes. The extent and severity of these changes are unchanged. Small fibrotic bands in the dorsobasal lungs, greater on the right.
Persistent solid pulmonary nodules, most of which are mildly decreased in size:
Pulmonary nodules in series 5 are as follows:
Right middle lobe:
- Solid nodule measuring 4 mm on image 165 (previously the same size).
Right lower lobe:
- Solid perifissural polygonal nodule measuring 6 mm on image 85 (previously the same size) - likely intrapulmonary lymph node.
- Solid nodule with lobulated margins measuring 5 mm on image 134 (previously 5 mm).
- Solid nodule with lobulated margins measuring 8 mm on image 217 (previously the same size).
Left lower lobe:
- Solid nodule with lobulated margins measuring 5 mm on image 85 (previously 5 mm).
- Polygonal subpleural nodule measuring 5 mm on image 143 (previously 5 mm).
No infiltrative changes in the lungs.
Fragmentary bronchial contents are seen within the lumens of bilateral subsegmental bronchi.
No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No significant pericardial effusion. Fluid is seen in the pericardial recesses. Coronary calcification is present in the left main, left anterior descending, and right coronary arteries, qualitatively assessed as moderate. Agatston score 166.

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is not dilated. Calcified plaques are seen in the walls of the aorta and proximal brachiocephalic arteries. Main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Degenerative changes of the spine in the imaged field.

Upper abdomen: Unremarkable.
rad_impression_en:
1. Moderate mixed emphysema, unchanged compared with CT on September 16, 2025.

2. CT signs of exudative bronchitis in the form of bilateral subsegmental mucus plugs, with interval development compared with CT on September 16, 2025.

3. Interstitial pulmonary changes with ground-glass opacity related to the underlying disease, unchanged compared with CT on September 16, 2025.

4. Pulmonary nodules in the right and left lungs measuring up to 7 mm, with decrease in size of a few nodules compared with CT on September 16, 2025. Per Fleischner Society recommendations, follow-up chest CT at 18-24 months is recommended for high-risk patients, and CT at 18-24 months may be considered for low-risk patients.

5. Incidental coronary artery calcification is present, qualitatively assessed as moderate. CAC-DRS A2/N3. Unchanged compared with CT on September 16, 2025. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for a comprehensive cardiovascular risk assessment or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

6. Atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta.

7. Moderate degenerative changes of the spine in the imaged field.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Heterogeneous lung aeration due to alternating areas of mosaic attenuation and areas of moderate paraseptal and centrilobular emphysema, unchanged. Multiple ill-defined centrilobular ground glass opacities, more pronounced in the upper zones. Secretion of small subsegmental bronchi, wall thickening and secretion of the segmental and subsegmental bronchi. Small fibrotic bands in the dorsobasal lungs, greater on the right. No pleural effusion.
Pulmonary nodules in series 5 are as follows:
Right upper lobe:
- Solid nodule measures 3 mm on image 47, unchanged.
Right middle lobe:
- Solid nodule measures 3 mm on image 132, unchanged.
- Solid nodule measures 4 mm on image 165, unchanged.
- Ground-glass nodule measures 4 mm on image 166, previously not seen. 
Right lower lobe:
- Solid perifissural polygonal nodule measures 6 mm on image 80, unchanged - likely intrapulmonary lymph node.
- Solid subpleural nodule measures 6 mm on image 85, unchanged.
- Solid nodule measures 5 mm on image 128, unchanged.
- Solid perifissural polygonal nodule measures 6 mm on image 130, unchanged - likely intrapulmonary lymph node.
- Solid subpleural nodule measures 5 mm on image 134 (previously 5 mm).
- Solid nodule measures 7 mm on image 182, unchanged.
- Solid nodule measures 8 mm on image 213, unchanged.
- Solid nodule measures 3 mm on image 206, unchanged.
- Ground-glass nodule measures 6 mm on image 213, previously not seen. 
Left upper lobe:
- Solid nodule measures 4 mm on image 47, unchanged.
- Part-solid nodule measures 3 mm on image 63, previously not seen. 
Left lower lobe:
- Calcified nodule measures 4 mm on image 79, unchanged.
- Subpleural solid nodule measures 5 mm on image 143, unchanged.
Multiple solid micronodules up to 2 mm, unchanged.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No significant pericardial effusion. Fluid is seen in the pericardial recesses. Coronary calcification is present in the left main, left anterior descending, and right coronary arteries, qualitatively assessed as moderate. 

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is not dilated. Calcified plaques are seen in the walls of the aorta and proximal brachiocephalic arteries. Main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Mild degenerative changes of the spine. Decrease in the density of the vertebral bodies T11-L2 to 67 HU (CT signs of demineralization).


Upper abdomen: Unremarkable.
val_impression_en:
1. Moderate mixed emphysema, unchanged compared with CT on September 16, 2025.

2. CT signs of exudative bronchitis in the form of bilateral subsegmental mucus plugs, with interval development compared with CT on September 16, 2025.

3. Interstitial pulmonary changes with ground-glass opacity related to the underlying disease, unchanged compared with CT on September 16, 2025.

4. Pulmonary nodules in the right and left lungs measuring up to 7 mm, with decrease in size of a few nodules compared with CT on September 16, 2025. Per Fleischner Society recommendations, follow-up chest CT at 18-24 months is recommended for high-risk patients, and CT at 18-24 months may be considered for low-risk patients.

5. Incidental coronary artery calcification is present, qualitatively assessed as moderate. CAC-DRS A2/N3. Unchanged compared with CT on September 16, 2025. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for a comprehensive cardiovascular risk assessment or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

6. Atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta.

7. Moderate degenerative changes of the spine in the imaged field.

rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Solid nodule with irregular margins measuring 1.5 × 1.0 cm in the right upper lobe (series 6, image 60), unchanged.
Linear atelectatic changes in the right middle lobe, lingular segments, and left basal segments.
Multiple peripheral micronodules adjacent to this nodule, unchanged.
No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly.
No significant pericardial effusion.
No coronary artery calcification.

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is not dilated.
Main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Unremarkable.

Upper abdomen: Unremarkable.
rad_impression_en: 1. Solid nodule in the right upper lobe measuring 1.5 × 1.0 cm with peripheral micronodules, stable compared with October 07, 2025. Given the history of suspected coccidioidomycosis on studies dated October 07, 2025 and December 22, 2023, and the presence of cavitary consolidation in the same region on the earlier study dated December 22, 2023, this lesion likely represents a postinfectious scar change (granuloma). In the absence of clinical symptoms, further CT follow-up is not required; alternatively, consider repeat CT in 12-24 months.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Solid nodule measuring 1.5 × 1.0 cm in the right upper lobe (series 6, image 60), with multiple adjacent micronodules up to 3 mm. No significant change is noted compared with the chest CT of 10/07/2025. Compared with the chest CT of 22/12/2023, the largest nodule has decreased in size, no cavitation is identified, and the number of micronodules has decreased. The previously described subsolid nodules (noted on the study from 12/22/2023) in the right lower lobe and left upper lobe are no longer visualized. Fleischner Society recommendations are not applicable.
No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No significant pericardial effusion. No coronary artery calcification.

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is not dilated. Main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Unremarkable.

Upper abdomen: Nonobstructing calculus (2 mm) in the upper pole of the right kidney.
val_impression_en:
1. Solid nodule in the right upper lobe with multiple adjacent micronodules. No significant change is noted compared with the chest CT of 10/07/2025. Compared with the chest CT of 12/22/2023, the largest nodule has decreased in size, no cavitation is identified, and the number of micronodules has decreased. Given the history of suspected coccidioidomycosis on studies dated October 07, 2025 and December 22, 2023, and the presence of cavitary consolidation in the same region on the earlier study dated December 22, 2023, this lesion likely represents a residual post-infectious nodule.

2. Right renal calculus.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.

rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Pulmonary nodules in series 5 are as follows:
Right upper lobe:
- Solid subpleural nodule measuring 3 mm on image 27 (unchanged).
Left upper lobe:
- Solid subpleural nodule measuring 4 mm on image 36 (unchanged).
In both lungs, solid micronodules measuring up to 2 mm (unchanged). Small bilateral apical "caps" are visualized (unchanged). Local pleural thickening in the right middle lobe, series 5, images 115-145 (unchanged). Calcified granulomas measuring up to 3 mm in both lungs (unchanged). Walls of some bronchi are unevenly thickened, with bronchial contents in the lumens of some bronchi. No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy. Calcifications are present in a few nonenlarged intrathoracic lymph nodes.

Heart: Cardiomegaly (cardiothoracic index 0.52). Mild pericardial effusion measuring up to 8 mm. Coronary calcification is present in the left main, left anterior descending, left circumflex, and right coronary arteries, qualitatively assessed as severe.

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is not dilated, with mild wall calcification. Main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Decreased density of vertebral bodies at the T1-T12 level up to +121 HU (signs of demineralization).

Upper abdomen: In the liver, calcifications measuring up to 2 mm.
rad_impression_en:
1. Micronodules in both lungs and solid nodules in both upper lobes, stable compared with CT dated September 03, 2025; per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations, additional CT follow-up is not recommended. Bronchial contents and bronchial wall thickening in both lungs, which may be consistent with bronchitis. Calcified granulomas in both lungs and calcification of intrathoracic lymph nodes, consistent with prior infection (unchanged compared with CT dated September 03, 2025). Small bilateral apical "caps" (unchanged compared with CT dated September 03, 2025).

2. Cardiomegaly. Mild pericardial effusion. Incidental coronary artery calcification is present, qualitatively assessed as severe. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for a comprehensive cardiovascular risk assessment or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

3. Mild atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta.

4. Liver calcifications. No further evaluation is required.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Pulmonary nodules in series 5 are as follows:
Right upper lobe:
- Solid subpleural nodule measures 3 mm on image 27, unchanged.
In both lungs, solid micronodules measure up to 2 mm, unchanged. Calcified granulomas measure up to 3 mm in both lungs, unchanged, and require no follow-up. Bilateral apical scarring, unchanged. Local pleural thickening in the right middle lobe, series 5, images 115-145, unchanged. Bronchial contents in the lumens and mild wall thickening of subsegmental bronchi of the lower lobes are present (for example, image 142 in the right anterior basal segment). No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy. Calcifications are present in a few nonenlarged intrathoracic lymph nodes.

Heart: The heart is slightly enlarged (cardiothoracic index 0.52). Trace pericardial effusion measuring up to 8 mm, unchanged. Coronary calcification is present in the left main, left anterior descending, left circumflex, and right coronary arteries, qualitatively assessed as severe.

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is not dilated, with mild wall calcification. Main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.

Upper abdomen: In the liver, calcifications measuring up to 2 mm. No further evaluation is required.
val_impression_en:
1. Solid nodules measure up to 3 mm in both lungs, stable compared with CT dated September 03, 2025, and May 14, 2024. ACR Fleischner Society recommendations (MacMahon, et al. Radiology 2017; 284(1): 228-43) suggest the following: No need for further follow-up for patients with high or low risk of lung cancer.

2. Calcified granulomas in both lungs and calcification of intrathoracic lymph nodes, consistent with prior infection, require no follow-up.

3. Bronchial contents and mild wall thickening of subsegmental bronchi of the lower lobes may be consistent with bronchitis.

4. Trace pericardial effusion, unchanged.

5. Incidental coronary artery calcification is present, qualitatively assessed as severe. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for a comprehensive cardiovascular risk assessment or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. Mild atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.

rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: In the left lower lobe, on image 70, series 5, there is a pleural-based part-solid nodule with spiculated margins, pleural tags extending to the interlobar and costal pleura, and a cystic component, overall measuring 2.4 × 1.8 cm; the solid component measures 8 × 6 mm.
Linear bands in the right lower lobe.
Polymorphous pleural-based opacities with irregular margins in the apical right upper lobe.
Accessory fissure in the right upper lobe.
No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly.
No significant pericardial effusion.
Coronary calcification is present in the left main, left anterior descending, left circumflex, and right coronary arteries, qualitatively assessed as mild.

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is not dilated, with mild wall calcification.
Main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the spine.
Schmorl's nodes of the adjacent endplates of T8 and T9.
Anterior wedge deformity of the T2 vertebral body with vertebral body height loss up to 30%.
Density of vertebral bodies is decreased to 100-130 HU (signs of demineralization).
Moderate bilateral gynecomastia is present.

Upper abdomen: Cystic lesion in the left kidney measuring 1.4 × 1.3 cm, with internal calcification.
rad_impression_en:
1. Complex nodule in the left lower lobe with features suspicious for neoplasm. Given the patient's high risk (age 75 years, long smoking history, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease), this finding, in accordance with the 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations, requires further evaluation with PET/CT and biopsy.

2. Incidental coronary artery calcification is present, qualitatively assessed as mild.
The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for a comprehensive cardiovascular risk assessment or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

3. Compression fracture of the T2 vertebral body (Genant grade 2).

4. Cystic lesion of the left kidney with internal calcification. Further evaluation with CT with intravenous contrast is recommended. Metallic clips in the gallbladder fossa.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: In the left lower lobe, on image 70, series 5, there is a subpleural part-solid nodule with spiculated margins, pleural tags extending to the interlobar and costal pleura, and pseudo-cavitation in the center, overall measuring 2.4 × 1.8 cm; the solid component measures 8 × 6 mm. Bilateral multiple apical subpleural solid micronodules (for example, on the right in images 37, 49, 57, 64 and on the left in images 41, 45, 49, 63). Linear scarring in the right lower lobe with traction bronchiectasis, unchanged compared to Abdomen CT on March 21, 2024. There are a few areas of paraseptal emphysema in the right apex. Bilateral apical scarring. No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy. Paratracheal lymph node up to 7 mm along the short axis.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No significant pericardial effusion. Coronary calcification is present in the left anterior descending, left circumflex, and right coronary arteries, qualitatively assessed as moderate. Mitral valve calcifications.

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is not dilated, with moderate wall calcification. Main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the spine. Biconcave deformity of the T2 vertebral body with vertebral body height loss up to 30% (Genant grade 2). Density of vertebral bodies is decreased to 100–130 HU, signs of demineralization, which is consistent with Bone Density Test on January 31, 2025.

Upper abdomen: Cystic lesion in the left kidney measuring 1.4 × 1.3 cm, with internal calcification, previously measuring 8 × 8 mm on Abdomen CT on March 21, 2024. Cholecystectomy.
val_impression_en:
1. Part-solid nodule in the left lower lobe measures 2.4 × 1.8 cm; the solid component measures 8 × 6 mm. ACR Fleischner Society recommendations (MacMahon, et al. Radiology 2017; 284(1): 228-43) suggest the following: Follow-up chest CT at 3-6 months to confirm persistence. If the nodule is persistent, consider PET/CT, tissue sampling, or surgical resection.
Multiple subpleural solid micronodules. ACR Fleischner Society recommendations (MacMahon, et al. Radiology 2017; 284(1): 228-43) suggest the following: For patients with high risk of lung cancer, generally no need for follow-up. An optional follow-up chest CT at 12 months could be performed if there is high index of suspicion. If unchanged, no further follow-up is necessary.

2. Mild paraseptal emphysema in the right apex. 
Pulmonary emphysema on CT is an independent risk factor for lung cancer. Consider enrollment in a low-dose CT (LDCT) lung cancer screening program if the patient meets eligibility criteria; today's CT may serve as a baseline for future screening. Note: Recommendation applies unless the patient has a history of lung cancer, known pulmonary nodules or lung mass, receives routine chest CTs/screening, or is receiving palliative care.

3. Incidental coronary artery calcification is present, qualitatively assessed as moderate. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for a comprehensive cardiovascular risk assessment or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. Mitral valve calcifications.

4. Cystic lesion of the left kidney with internal calcification, with interval worsening due to increase in lesion size. ACR White Paper recommendations (Herts, et al. J Am Coll Radiol 2018;15:264-273) suggest the following: Further evaluation with CT or MRI without and with IV contrast recommended.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
